LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK'S MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CLAIM FOR MALICE, OPPRESSION OR FRAUD ARISING OUT OF A BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED AS TO PARAGRAPH 15 WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED AS TO PARAGRAPHS 17, 21, 22, 24, AND 28 AS THEY DO NOT CONFLICT WITH THE COURT'S ORDER ON APRIL 19, 2013 AND ARE NOT IRRELEVANT, FALSE, OR IMPROPER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS ALLEGING BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY IN RELATION TO DEFENDANT'S RATE INCREASE WITHOUT CLIENT CONSENT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS IT RELATES TO THE SECOND, THIRD, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED-AS HE MUST--THAT HE WAS ACTUALLY INNOCENT OF THE UNDERLYING CRIME AND DID NOT OPPOSE THE DEMURRER AS IT RELATED TO THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS IT RELATES TO THE SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH, AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE BREACH OF CONTRACT, BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTIES, BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING, AND/OR FRAUD/NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION THAT DO NOT IMPLICATE THE QUALITY OF THE REPRESENTATION PROVIDED IN THE UNDERLYING LITIGATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOLLOWING PORTIONS OF THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT : A) PAGE 2, PARAGRAPH 6 OF THE SAC IN ITS ENTIRETY; B) PAGE 2 , PAR. 8 OF THE SAC IN ITS ENTIRETY ; C) PAGE 3 , PAR. 9 OF THE SAC IN ITS ENTIRETY; D) PAGE 4, PAR. 14 OF THE SAC THE TERMS, "FOR SERVICES NOT PERFORMED AND FOR THE HIGHER RATE WHICH WAS NOT COMMENSURATE WITH BLANK'S REAL EXPERIENCE AND SKILLS; E) PAGE 5, PAR. 22 OF THE SAC THE TERMS "THAT DEFENDANTS FAILED TO DISCLSE ASPECTS OF THE SUBJECT CRIMINAL LITIGATION AND THAT DEFENDANT BLANK HAD MISLED PLAINTIFF ABOUT THE NATURE OF HIS CRIMINAL JURY TRIAL EXPERIENCE IN ORDER TO CONVINCE PLAINTIFF THAT DEFENDANT BLANK HAD SUFFICIENT EXPERIENCE AND SAVVY TO REPRESENT TOM IN THE UNDERLYING MATTER."; F) PAGE 6 , PAR. 24 OF THE SAC , THE TERM, "DEFENDANTS INFORMED PLAINTIFF IN WRITING THAT DEFENDANTS WOULD SECURE AND RETAIN EXPERT WITNESSES TO TESTIFY ON BEHALF OF PLAINTIFF IN THE UNDERLYING MATTER; G) PAGE 6, PAR. 25 OF THE SAC IN ITS ENTIRETY; H) PAGE 6 , PAR. 26 OF THE SAC IN ITS ENTIRETY; I) PAGE 7 , PAR. 34 OF THE SAC IN ITS ENTIRETY; J) PAGE 7, PAR. 35 OF THE SAC IN ITS ENTIRETY; K) THE PRAYER AT PAGE 9, SUBPAR. 4 IN ITS ENTIRETY; L) THE PRAYER AT PAGE 9 SUB. 5, IN ITS ENTIRETY. REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. THE PRAYER AT PAGE 9, SUBPARAGRAPH 6, IN ITS ENTIRETY IS STRICKEN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302)

No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JEREMY D. BLANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant met his initial burden showing there was no breach of fiduciary duty, no causation, and no damages. (Undisputed Material Facts, 1-18.) No opposition filed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer is overruled as to the first cause of action. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts alleging breach of fiduciary duty in relation to defendant's rate increase without client consent. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as it relates to the second, third, and fourth causes of action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK's MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied in part and granted in part. The motion to strike is denied as to punitive damages. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to state a claim for malice, oppression or fraud arising out of a breach of fiduciary duty. The motion to strike is granted as to paragraph 15 without leave to amend. The motion to strike is denied as to paragraphs 17, 21, 22, 24, and 28 as they do not conflict with the court's order on April 19, 2013 and are not irrelevant, false, or improper. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RUSSELL A. ROBINSON, TEL. NO. 255-0462 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DAVID B. OWEN OF THE NEMECEK.COLE LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (818) 788-9500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS granted WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOLLOWING PORTIONS OF THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT : A) PAGE 2, PARAGRAPH 6 OF THE SAC IN ITS ENTIRETY; B) Page 2 , Par. 8 of the SAC in its entirety ; C) Page 3 , Par. 9 of the SAC in its entirety; D) Page 4, par. 14 of the SAC the terms, "for services not performed and for the higher rate which was not commensurate with Blank's real experience and skills; E) Page 5, par. 22 of the SAC the terms "That defendants failed to disclse aspects of the subject criminal litigation and that Defendant Blank had misled Plaintiff about the nature of his criminal jury trial experience in order to convince Plaintiff that Defendant Blank had sufficient experience and savvy to represent Tom in the underlying matter."; F) Page 6 , Par. 24 of the SAC , the term, "Defendants informed Plaintiff in writing that Defendants would secure and retain expert witnesses to testify on behalf of Plaintiff in the underlying matter; G) Page 6, Par. 25 of the SAC in its entirety; H) Page 6 , Par. 26 of the SAC in its entirety; I) Page 7 , Par. 34 of the SAC in its entirety; J) Page 7, Par. 35 of the SAC in its entirety; K) The prayer at Page 9, subpar. 4 in its entirety; L) The prayer at Page 9 sub. 5, in its entirety. Request for Judicial Notice is denied. The prayer at page 9, subparagraph 6, in its entirety is STRICKEN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT TO BE FILED WITHOUT THE STRICKEN LANGUAGE WITHIN 10 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. DEFENDANT DOES NOT WAIVE ANY DEFENSES TO NEW DEMURRER. Off Calendar Apr-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action. Plaintiff has not pled-as he must--that he was actually innocent of the underlying crime and did not oppose the demurrer as it related to this cause of action. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as it relates to the second, third, fourth, and fifth causes of action. Plaintiff is granted 10 days leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege breach of contract, breach of fiduciary duties, breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing, and/or fraud/negligent misrepresentation that do not implicate the quality of the representation provided in the underlying litigation. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
